projects.name: Traffic Light System
projects.desc: A traffic light controller built on Arduino Uno using three LEDs (Red, Yellow, Green) on a breadboard, each driven through a current-limiting resistor. Digital output pins sequence the light phases with timed delays, simulating a real-world intersection signal cycle.

projects.name: Vehicle Alarm System
projects.desc: Vehicle intrusion alarm using an Arduino Uno with a force/pressure sensor to detect impact or tampering on the vehicle body. A potentiometer sets the sensitivity threshold; when triggered, a piezo buzzer sounds the alarm. Alarm state and sensor readings are shown on a 16×2 I2C LCD, with an LED as a visual armed/alert indicator.

projects.name: Smart Irrigation System
projects.desc: Automated irrigation system using a resistive soil moisture sensor connected to Arduino's analog input. A relay module switches a 9V-powered DC water pump motor based on moisture thresholds. Green and Red LEDs indicate pump on/off status, with manual override via a push button.

projects.name: Parking Detection Device
projects.desc: Reverse parking proximity alarm using an HC-SR04 ultrasonic sensor to continuously measure the distance to obstacles behind the vehicle. Distance readings are shown in real time on a 20×4 I2C LCD. As the vehicle approaches an object, a piezo buzzer beeps at increasing frequency and a Red LED lights up to warn the driver before a collision occurs.

projects.name: Voting Machine
projects.desc: Electronic voting unit built on Arduino Uno with 4 dedicated push-button candidates on a breadboard, each paired with a pull-down resistor. Vote tallies are shown on a 20×4 I2C LCD. Red and Green LEDs confirm vote registration, and firmware enforces a one-vote-per-session lock.

projects.name: Irrigation Pump for Pot
projects.desc: Compact indoor plant watering system using a resistive soil moisture probe connected to Arduino's analog input. A piezo buzzer alerts when soil is critically dry. A Red LED indicates pump status, and a push button provides manual override. Threshold adjustment is done via code configuration.

projects.name: Fire Alarm System
projects.desc: Dual-sensor fire detection system combining an MQ-series gas/smoke sensor and an LM35 temperature sensor, with readings displayed on a 16×2 LCD. A potentiometer sets the alarm threshold; on trigger, a piezo buzzer sounds and a relay activates an external siren or sprinkler. A Green LED indicates system-armed status.

projects.name: Reprogrammable Output Voltage Display
projects.desc: Precision voltage measurement and display unit using Arduino's 10-bit ADC and a potentiometer-based voltage divider for input scaling. Three push buttons allow reprogramming of display parameters and alert thresholds on-the-fly. Readings are shown on a 4-digit 7-segment HT16K33 backpack display via I2C.

projects.name: PIR Motion Sensing Device
projects.desc: Passive infrared motion sensing device using an HC-SR501 PIR sensor with onboard adjustable sensitivity and time-delay trimmers. Detected motion triggers a relay output and is timestamped by an RTC module. A large LDR resistor array on the breadboard provides ambient light level sensing to enable day/night aware detection logic.

projects.name: Solar Tracking System
projects.desc: Dual-axis solar panel tracker using 4 LDRs arranged in a cross configuration on a breadboard to detect light intensity differential. Two servo motors (horizontal and vertical) are controlled via a threshold-based algorithm (posH/posV angle variables) to align the panel toward peak sunlight. A multimeter measures harvested panel output current in real time. Simulated and validated in Tinkercad with full Arduino sketch.
